result:
I think I'd want everyone to have genuine curiosity about perspectives different from their own. Not necessarily agreement, but that initial instinct to wonder "what experiences led them to see it that way?" rather than immediately dismissing what seems foreign or wrong.

So much conflict and missed opportunity seems to come from our tendency to build walls around our own views. But imagine if our first reaction was to lean in and ask questions instead - how much more we might learn, how many problems we could solve together, how much unnecessary suffering we could avoid.

What change would you most want to see?

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
